varieties.name: Barhee (Barhi) 🏆
varieties.origin: Basra region, Iraq (ancient cultivation, 1000+ years)
varieties.traits: Medium-height palm (5-10m), heavy trunk with broad leaf bases, long and wide fruit stalks. Named after 'barh' meaning 'hot wind' in Arabic.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, soft, extremely sweet with honey-like, butterscotch, and caramel flavor. Often called 'Honey Dates' or 'Honey Balls'. Some consider it the sweetest of all date varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding: 6-20 bunches per tree, each bunch 8-15kg. Can produce 80-125kg per mature tree annually.
varieties.availability: Available from Australian date palm nurseries including Gurra Downs (www.gurradowns.com.au), Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, and tissue culture suppliers in Queensland. Widely planted in commercial date farms across Australia.

varieties.name: Medjool (Medjul) 🏆
varieties.origin: Bou Denib Oasis, Morocco (ancient variety). Only 11 offshoots imported to USA in 1927 to escape Bayoud disease, forming basis of all modern Medjool cultivation outside Morocco.
varieties.traits: Medium to large palm (up to 20-25m), thick canopy with dense silvery-green to dark green foliage. Wide leaflets create lush, full appearance. Fronds 9-15 feet long. Genetic analysis shows natural hybrid between Phoenix dactylifera and Phoenix theophrasti.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very soft, fleshy, moist, sticky texture. Rich, intensely sweet with caramel and honey notes. Premium dessert quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high: 75-100kg per mature tree. Consistent producer.

varieties.name: Deglet Noor (Deglet Nur) 🏆
varieties.origin: Algerian Sahara near Touggourt, Algeria (1600s). Name means 'Date of Light' in Arabic. Imported to California 1900, introduced to Australia late 1800s-early 1900s.
varieties.traits: Medium to tall (12-25m), loose crown with fewer fronds than Medjool, creating open, airy canopy. Silvery blue-green leaves up to 4.5m long. More sparse appearance. Like Medjool, genetic analysis shows natural hybrid with Phoenix theophrasti.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, chewy texture with delicate, mild honey-like sweetness. Dryer and less sweet than Medjool. Excellent for cooking, baking, and chopping due to firmer texture.
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding: 90-135kg (200-300 lbs) per mature tree in ideal conditions.

varieties.name: Halawi (Halawy, Holwah) 🏆
varieties.origin: Iraq (ancient variety, name means 'sweet' in Arabic). One of original varieties brought from Middle East to California. Thousands of years of cultivation history.
varieties.traits: Even larger palm than Medjool - can reach 20m+ height. Vigorous growth habit. Dense foliage.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, delicate, tender flesh with extremely sweet caramel or honey-like flavor. Rich, almost melting texture. Often described as having chocolate/fig-like sweetness.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate: 60-80kg per mature tree.

varieties.name: Hayani (Hayany) 🏆
varieties.origin: Egypt (ancient variety, name is a man's name in Arabic). Most extensively planted cultivar in Egypt. Introduced to California 1901.
varieties.traits: One of the most cold-tolerant date palms. Medium-sized tree with good vigor.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, meaty flesh with mild, sweet flavor. Best consumed fresh as it is not easy to cure/dry.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good productivity. Available year-round in Egypt.

varieties.name: Khadrawy (Khadrawi) 🏆
varieties.origin: Basra, Iraq (ancient cultivation). Introduced to USA early 1900s. Thousands of years of cultivation history.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized palm with characteristic mahogany-colored appearance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, meaty flesh with sweet, distinctive chocolate/fig-like, caramel flavor. Rich sweetness. High moisture content.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate: 60-80kg per tree.

varieties.name: Dayri 🏆
varieties.origin: Basra, Iraq (ancient variety). Semi-dry type. Naturalized to California and other regions.
varieties.traits: Medium to large palm, vigorous growth.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, soft flesh with sweet, unique slightly savory undertone. Rich flavor profile. High fiber content.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good productivity.

varieties.name: Thoory (Thuri) 🏆
varieties.origin: Algeria (ancient cultivation). Known as 'Bread Date' due to dry texture. Traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Stout palm with short, stiff leaves. Heavy bearing habit. Very large clusters.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dry type - sometimes hard and brittle but good, sweet, nutty flavor. Firm, chewy texture. Not sticky. Can be carried in pocket - traditional nomadic food.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - heavy bearing variety with very large clusters.
varieties.availability: Uncommon in Australia. May be available through specialist importers or propagated on special order.

varieties.name: Sayer (Sair, Shamran) 🏆
varieties.origin: Iran/Middle East, particularly Khuzestan and Bushehr regions. Very old variety, widely cultivated.
varieties.traits: Large, vigorous palm. One of the major commercial varieties in Iran.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, semi-dry texture with rich caramel-like flavor and hint of nuttiness. Sweet but not overly cloying. Easy to pit.
varieties.yield_notes: Good to high productivity.

varieties.name: Khastawi (Khusatawi) 🏆
varieties.origin: Iraq (leading soft date variety in Iraq). Ancient cultivation.

varieties.name: Barhee (Barhi) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Basra, Iraq (ancient). Now extensively cultivated in India, particularly Andhra Pradesh, Gujarat, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, and increasingly in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Medium-height (5-10m), heavy trunk, broad leaf bases. Thrives in Mediterranean, desert, subtropical, and tropical climates.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, extremely sweet with honey, butterscotch, caramel flavors. Sweetest variety. 'Honey Balls' or 'Honey Dates'.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high: 6-20 bunches/tree, 8-15kg/bunch. Total 80-125kg annually per mature tree. Developed by ICAR in India in 2016.

varieties.name: Medjool (Medjul) 🏆
varieties.origin: Morocco. Now cultivated in limited quantities in India (Rajasthan, Gujarat, parts of Tamil Nadu).
varieties.traits: Large palm (20-25m), dense silvery-green foliage, thick canopy. Hardy in subtropical/tropical zones 9-11.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very soft, moist, sticky with rich caramel-honey flavor. Premium quality.
varieties.yield_notes: 75-100kg per mature tree. Good consistency.

varieties.name: Halawi (Halawy) 🏆
varieties.origin: Iraq (ancient, name means 'sweet' in Arabic). One of oldest varieties imported to USA. Grown in India.
varieties.traits: Very large palm (20m+), vigorous, dense foliage.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, delicate, melting texture with extremely sweet caramel-honey flavor. Rich, almost chocolate/fig notes.
varieties.yield_notes: 60-80kg per tree. Listed in Indian cultivation guides.

varieties.name: Khunezi (Khuneji) 🌱
varieties.origin: Middle East. Cultivated in India, particularly Rajasthan and other date-growing states. Early maturing variety.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized palm, adapted to Indian growing conditions.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deliciously intensely sweet. High quality fruit.
varieties.yield_notes: Average 40kg per tree (moderate).
varieties.availability: Available from Indian nurseries, particularly in Rajasthan date palm programs. Agricultural universities in Gujarat and Rajasthan supply this variety.

varieties.name: Khalas 🌱
varieties.origin: Saudi Arabia (Al-Ahsa/Huffuf region - famous cultivation area). Name means 'quintessence' in Arabic. Now grown in India.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized palm, cultivated in Rajasthan and other Indian date-growing states.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, moist, sticky with hearty butter-caramel taste. Medium sweetness level - not too high, not too low, suits most people. Rich, toffee-like flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Premium cultivar with mid-season ripening.
varieties.availability: Available from Indian date palm nurseries in Rajasthan, Gujarat. Listed in Indian agricultural programs.

varieties.name: Khadrawy (Khadrawi) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Iraq (ancient). Listed in Indian cultivation as 'Khadarawy'. Grown in Indian date programs.
varieties.traits: Medium palm, adapted to Indian growing conditions.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, yellow flesh, sweet with chocolate/fig-like caramel notes. High moisture content.
varieties.yield_notes: 60-80kg per tree. Listed for 'Chaharamaking' processing.

varieties.name: Zahidi (Zahdi) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Ancient Mesopotamia/Iraq. Extensively cultivated in India (Rajasthan, Gujarat, other states). Listed as 'Wild date palm' in some Indian references.
varieties.traits: Stout, fast-growing with silvery-green foliage. Successfully grown throughout India.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Semi-dry, very sugary, mild sweetness with nutty/peanut butter notes. Firm, chewy.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - up to 136kg per tree. Heavy bearer.

varieties.name: Shamran (Sayer) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Iran/Middle East. Listed in Indian cultivation guides as one of recommended varieties. Widely grown in Persian Gulf countries.
varieties.traits: Large, vigorous palm. Major commercial variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, semi-dry with rich caramel flavor and nutty hints. Heavy, sweet.
varieties.yield_notes: Good to high productivity.

varieties.name: Khastawi 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Iraq - leading soft date in Iraq. Ancient cultivation. Grown in India.
varieties.traits: Large, vigorous palm adapted to desert conditions but with humidity tolerance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Syrupy, extremely sweet. Prized for dessert.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity.

varieties.name: Dayri 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Basra, Iraq. Listed in Indian agricultural guides as semi-dry variety suitable for cultivation.
varieties.traits: Medium-large palm, vigorous growth.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, soft with sweet, slightly savory flavor. High fiber content.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good productivity.

varieties.name: Thoory 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Algeria. 'Bread Date'. Listed in Indian cultivation guides as dry variety.
varieties.traits: Stout with short stiff leaves, heavy bearing, very large clusters.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dry, sometimes hard but good sweet nutty flavor. Not sticky.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high with very large clusters.

varieties.name: Ajwa (Ajwah) 🏆
varieties.origin: Al-Madinah region, Saudi Arabia. Considered holy fruit in Islamic tradition with spiritual significance.

varieties.name: Safawi
varieties.origin: Madinah, Saudi Arabia. Productive variety available year-round.

varieties.name: Phoenix sylvestris (Indian Wild Date Palm) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to India - found throughout most of India including tropical monsoon climates region.
varieties.traits: Indigenous Indian palm species, smaller fruit than Phoenix dactylifera.

varieties.name: Monitor for fungal diseases during humid summer periods
varieties.availability: Source from Queensland nurseries including Gurra Downs, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Advanced Palms Australia
